feed.title: Google Cloud: Managed Service for Apache Airflow
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Managed Service for Apache Airflow

entry.content: New Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments created while the Restrict Endpoint Usage organization policy is active now use regional endpoints for services like Cloud Storage, Cloud Logging, Pub/Sub, and Data Lineage. For more information, see Configure environments with Restrict Endpoint Usage policy.
entry.summary: New Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments, when the Restrict Endpoint Usage policy is active, will now utilize regional endpoints for key services. This change enhances security and compliance by directing service traffic through regional endpoints. Learn more about configuring these environments in our documentation.
entry.title: Feature: Managed Airflow Now Uses Regional Endpoints

entry.content:
Several API dependencies that aren't required by Managed Airflow (Gen 3) are now phased out and must be enabled separately if you want to create Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments in a new project. This change was announced previously.

The following API dependencies were phased out:

  • artifactregistry.googleapis.com
  • cloudbuild.googleapis.com
  • container.googleapis.com
  • pubsub.googleapis.com

The following API dependencies aren't phased out yet and are scheduled to be detached from the Cloud Composer API in the future:

  • sqladmin.googleapis.com

Existing Managed Airflow (Gen 3) and Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments in projects where the Cloud Composer API is already enabled aren't impacted.

You can do the following:

  • If your project has only Managed Airflow (Gen 3) environments, then you can manually disable the listed APIs that were phased out.
  • If your project has Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments, then we recommend to keep these APIs enabled because disabling them might lead to environment's malfunction.
  • If you want to create Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments in a new project, you can enable the listed APIs manually or using a Google Cloud CLI command. For more information, see Enable Managed Airflow (Gen 2) dependencies.
  • If you use automation scripts to provision Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments, then make sure that the listed APIs are enabled in addition to the Cloud Composer API.
entry.summary: Several API dependencies are no longer required for Managed Airflow (Gen 3) and have been phased out. If you create new Managed Airflow (Gen 2) environments, you may need to enable these APIs separately. Existing environments are not affected by this change.
entry.title: Change: API dependencies for Managed Airflow updated

entry.content: (Managed Airflow Gen 3) You can now access Cloud Run endpoints restricted to internal ingress traffic through your environment's network attachment. This feature is available through gcloud CLI beta commands and beta Cloud Composer API in all Managed Airflow (Gen 3) versions.
entry.summary: Managed Airflow (Gen 3) now supports accessing internal-only Cloud Run endpoints via your environment's network attachment. This new capability is available through gcloud CLI beta commands and the beta Cloud Composer API across all Gen 3 versions.
entry.title: Feature: Internal Cloud Run Access for Managed Airflow

entry.content:
Managed Service for Apache Airflow now supports Google Cloud tags for environments.

Tags provide a way to create annotations for resources, and conditionally allow or deny policies based on whether a resource has a specific tag.
entry.summary: Managed Service for Apache Airflow environments now support Google Cloud tags. Tags allow you to create annotations for resources and conditionally allow or deny policies based on tag presence.
entry.title: Feature: Airflow environments now support tags

entry.content: In Managed Airflow (Gen 3), it is now possible to create Kubernetes Secrets with the kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson secret type through the beta Cloud Composer API, in addition to the default Opaque secret type. For more information, see Manage Kubernetes Secrets.
entry.summary: Cloud Composer now supports creating Kubernetes Secrets of type `kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson` via the Cloud Composer API. This new feature expands the types of secrets you can manage for your Airflow environments.
entry.title: Feature: Kubernetes secrets support in Composer

entry.content: (Airflow 3) The INFO log level filter in Airflow UI now correctly displays log messages with this logging level.
entry.summary: The INFO log level filter in the Airflow UI has been fixed and now correctly displays log messages at this level. This resolves a previous issue where messages were not appearing as expected.
entry.title: Fixed: Airflow UI log filter fixed

entry.content:
New Airflow builds are available in Managed Airflow (Gen 3):

  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.10
  • composer-3-airflow-2.11.1-build.6 (default)
  • composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.39
entry.summary: New Airflow builds are now available for Managed Airflow (Gen 3). These updates include support for Airflow versions 3.1.7, 2.11.1 (default), and 2.10.5. You can find more details and specific build links in the documentation.
entry.title: Change: New Airflow builds available

entry.summary: New images for Managed Airflow (Gen 2) are now available, including composer-2.17.3-airflow-2.11.1 as the default. These updates bring the latest Airflow versions to your managed environments.
entry.title: Change: Managed Airflow image update

entry.content: The following Managed Airflow versions and builds have reached their end of support period: composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.24, composer-2.13.2-airflow-2.9.3, composer-2.13.2-airflow-2.10.5.
entry.summary: Several Managed Airflow versions are no longer supported. Please upgrade to a supported version to ensure continued service.
entry.title: Deprecated: Managed Airflow versions deprecated

entry.content: The [scheduler]print_stats_interval Airflow configuration option can now be overridden in environments with Airflow 2.10.5 and later. This option previously was blocked.
entry.summary: The `[scheduler]print_stats_interval` Airflow configuration option can now be overridden in environments with Airflow 2.10.5 and later. This option was previously blocked, but is now available for customization.
entry.title: Change: Airflow scheduler stats override enabled

entry.content:
New Airflow builds are available in Managed Airflow (Gen 3):

  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.9
  • composer-3-airflow-2.11.1-build.5 (default)
  • composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.38
entry.summary: New Airflow builds are now available for Managed Airflow (Gen 3). These updates include newer versions of Airflow, offering potential performance and security improvements. The default build now uses Airflow 2.11.1.
entry.title: Change: New Airflow builds available

entry.summary: New images for Managed Airflow (Gen 2) are now available, including the latest versions of composer and airflow. These updates bring improved features and stability to your Airflow environments.
entry.title: Change: New Managed Airflow Images Available

entry.content: The following Managed Airflow versions and builds have reached their end of support period: composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.23, composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.3, and composer-2.13.1-airflow-*.
entry.summary: Several Managed Airflow versions and builds are nearing their end of support. This includes specific composer-3 and composer-2 builds. Please refer to the versioning overview for details on deprecation and support timelines.
entry.title: Deprecated: Managed Airflow Versions Reaching End of Support

entry.content:
The google-api-core preinstalled package versions from 2.28.0 to 2.30.2 might cause degraded environment performance, which can result in longer times to execute a task and longer times to move a task from the queued to the executing state.

Affected Managed Airflow (Gen 3) builds:

  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.0 to composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.5
  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.0-build.5 to composer-3-airflow-3.1.0-build.10
  • composer-3-airflow-2.11.1-build.0
  • composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.22 to composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.33
  • composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.42 to composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.53

Affected Managed Airflow (Gen 2) builds:

  • composer-2.16.10-airflow-2.11.1
  • composer-2.16.0-airflow-2.10.5 to composer-2.16.10-airflow-2.10.5
  • composer-2.16.0-airflow-2.9.3 to composer-2.16.10-airflow-2.9.3

We recommend to upgrade your environment to the following versions, which contain a version of the package where the problem is fixed or isn't present:

  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.7 and later
  • composer-3-airflow-2.11.1-build.3 and later
  • composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.36 and later
  • composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.54 (contains 2.27.0)
  • composer-2.17.0-airflow-2.11.1 and later
  • composer-2.17.0-airflow-2.10.5 and later
  • composer-2.16.11-airflow-2.11.1 (contains 2.27.0)
  • composer-2.16.11-airflow-2.10.5 (contains 2.27.0)
  • composer-2.16.11-airflow-2.9.3 (contains 2.27.0)

As a workaround, you can manually install a later version of the google-api-core package to an affected environment by specifying >=2.30.3 as the required version.
entry.summary: Certain versions of the google-api-core package may cause degraded environment performance, leading to longer task execution and queuing times. We recommend upgrading to recommended versions or applying a workaround by manually installing a later version of the package.
entry.title: Issue: Environment Performance Degradation

entry.content:
New Airflow builds are available in Managed Airflow (Gen 3):

  • composer-3-airflow-3.1.7-build.8
  • composer-3-airflow-2.11.1-build.4 (default)
  • composer-3-airflow-2.10.5-build.37
entry.summary: New builds for Airflow 3.1.7, 2.11.1, and 2.10.5 are now available in Managed Airflow (Gen 3). The default build has been updated to Airflow 2.11.1.
entry.title: Change: New Airflow Builds Available

entry.summary: New images for Managed Airflow (Gen 2) are now available, including composer-2.17.1-airflow-2.11.1 as the default. These updates provide the latest versions for your Airflow environments.
entry.title: Change: New Managed Airflow Images Available

entry.content: The following Managed Airflow versions and builds have reached their end of support period: composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.22 and composer-2.13.0-airflow-*.
entry.summary: Certain versions of Managed Airflow, including composer-3-airflow-2.9.3-build.22 and composer-2.13.0-airflow-*, are nearing their end of support. Users are advised to review the versioning overview for details on deprecation and support timelines.
entry.title: Deprecated: Managed Airflow versions reaching end of support
